Everything that has gone down, and what is coming next
Vol. 1, No. 1ConfidentialJune 2026
The last twelve weeks

We did not slow down. We doubled down.

New business · March 1 to June 2, 2026

About twelve weeks ago Lisa moved on. We did not miss a beat. We doubled down, and in the weeks since we built almost all of this out: one of the strongest quarters in this agency's history, a rebuilt back office, new people on the team, and technology that did not exist before. Everything in this briefing happened in that window. This is the pace now, and nothing is stopping this train.

$0
New premium
0
Policies bound
0
Households
$0
Avg per deal
Safeshield, new premium pace (annualized)~$2.6M / yr
What a typical agency writes in a full year~$250K / yr
In one quarter we wrote about 2.6 times what a typical agency writes in an entire year.

Industry guideposts: agency networks describe roughly $250,000 in new premium as a normal full year, and about five years to build a $1,000,000 book at 90 percent retention (Tague Alliance and SIAA). Most agents need three to five years just to build a book that supports a living (InsuredMine). We are running near ten times that yearly pace, and we crossed a $3.6M book in under three years. Sources at the bottom of the page.

Carrier desk

Where the premium landed

By premium written this quarter, three markets led the way. That is healthy concentration with strong carriers, not premium scattered across the surplus channel.

20%
Progressive
Top carrier by volume
13%
Travelers
Personal and package lines
11%
Hartford
Commercial strength

Volume earns access. Carriers reward clean, profitable books with appointments and better terms, and our numbers are doing exactly that. We have opened doors with markets like Narragansett Bay and Kingstone, and we keep standing taller with Progressive. Most scratch agencies never get this. They write through wholesalers and give up half their commission. We are building direct.

The CASEY files

Take some of CASEY's new features for a spin

These are a few of the newest pieces we have shipped. Tap a tab to move through CASEY, then tap any item marked with a gold dot to see what it does.

CASEY
casey.gosafeshield.com
Outbox
Casey Jones, our client concierge, keeps every client warm on autopilot, from casey@gosafeshield.com.
CJ
Casey Jones Client Concierge
casey@gosafeshield.com
Sent automatically
Your renewal is coming up
Hi there, it is Casey. I told you I would be back when your renewal came around, and here I am. Your agent has already started reviewing your policy. Rates across the industry are up around 13 percent this year, so we are shopping our 68-plus carriers to keep you in the best spot. Anything change? Just reply and a real person will pick it up.
Casey Jones · Client Concierge · Safeshield Insurance
When a new client comes on, Casey runs an eight-email welcome over the first ninety days: onboarding and what to expect, what we cover, review and referral asks, an introduction to Austin and Eric, our content and socials, and a close-out that previews renewal time. Nobody sends a thing by hand.
Around renewal, Casey sends three: a heads-up that sets expectations on the rate environment (the industry is running near 13 percent), a confirmation once it renews, and a cross-sell and coverage review. That is eleven automated touchpoints per client every year.
The automation knows when to step back. Multiple opens, repeat clicks, or any reply pulls a client out of the sequence and routes them straight to a person, so a warm client always lands with one of us instead of an autoresponder.
Chat
Ask CASEY anything, in plain English.
CASEY is the platform we own and built. Ask it to draft a renewal email, log a deal, pull a client, or tell you the best carrier for a risk, and it does the work so the team can spend time selling and serving instead of digging through systems.
People
Every client and policy, in one clean view.
We pulled the entire book into CASEY's own database: 1,583 clients and 1,274 policies, fully ours. NowCerts still runs alongside it for now, so nothing was lost. The People tab is simply a cleaner, faster way to see a client and everything tied to them without fighting the old system to find it.
A full read and write connection to our AMS through 17 connected tools. Before anything writes back, CASEY shows a confirmation card so a person reviews the change first. Nothing commits by accident.
Documents
Drop a dec page in, get a finished policy out.
Snap a photo of a dec page or upload the PDF and CASEY reads it. It pulls the carrier, policy number, line of business, premium, effective and expiration dates, and the full coverage schedule, then auto-fills the Add Policy form and the Renewals flyout. No manual entry. NowCerts does not even do this, and it is a lifesaver when a carrier does not send Ivans downloads.
Carriers
Know exactly where to place a risk.
Data-driven carrier ratings and hot-carrier sorting, built off our own book rather than someone else's averages. When the team is deciding where to place a risk, CASEY points to the markets that are actually working for us right now.
Automations
The quiet work that runs without anyone touching it.
A bot pulls our AL3 carrier files from the Ivans Exchange every single day, with nobody babysitting it. Downloads land on their own.
Carrier login codes that hit the main mailbox are sorted and routed automatically to where they are needed, so quoting and servicing never stall waiting on a verification code.
Producer splits now live inside the system as consistent rules instead of spreadsheets and memory. Statements run against the same logic every time.
BrandNew
Proposals · Now live

You can edit any proposal yourself, in seconds

The biggest proposal upgrade yet just went live. After CASEY builds a proposal, you no longer have to ask it to rebuild the whole thing to change a word. Tap Edit, and the proposal opens right inside CASEY. Click any headline, price, or sentence and type over it. Add a photo of the client's home from your phone. Hit Save and Publish, and it is live in about thirty seconds. Made a mistake? Undo. You really cannot break it. And the annoying stuff is fixed too: building no longer feels frozen, and long proposals do not get cut off halfway.

Tap Edit and the proposal opens right over your chat. Click anything to change it.
Four one-tap themes, including a clean light version for printing.
Every proposal the team has built, in one place to reuse.
Hide the price, reorder sections

Switch to Layout mode to move sections up or down, or hide them entirely. Do not want to show the price on this one? Hide it with one tap. Perfect for showing the price to one client but not the next.

Make it stronger

A quick checklist offers add-ons like why this carrier fits, payment options, and bundle savings. Check what you want and CASEY adds only those sections.

Clients tap Questions and the note emails whoever built the proposal, with a central backup so no lead slips.
It all works from your phone. Save and Publish stays within thumb's reach.

Thirty-two client proposals built so far, every one of them deploying automatically to quotes.gosafeshield.com.

Where we tighten up

The number we are chasing next: 92

We are writing new business at a rare pace. The next frontier is keeping more of it. Our retention sits at 86 percent. The industry average is about 84 percent, so we are already ahead of the field, and the top-tier agencies live at 92 percent and up. That is the line we are drawing. Closing the gap from 86 to 92 is real money, and it is winnable.

Industry avg 84%We are here 86%Goal 92%
0%100%
Watch every renewal

Stay on top of renewals across the board, personal and commercial. Make sure surplus-lines policies renew on time, because those are the ones that quietly fall through if no one is looking.

Get ahead of our clients

Reach out before there is ever a problem. A quick check-in, a coverage review, a friendly call. Keep clients happy and protect the book we worked so hard to build.

Retention benchmarks: industry average about 84 percent, top performers 93 to 95 percent, and buyers treat 92 percent and up as top-tier (AgencyBloc; Wexford Insurance). Sources below.

ComingSoon
On the horizon

PL Rating in CASEY, almost here

This is the one I have been buried in for weeks. I am building real-time comparative quoting directly into CASEY, so you will be able to enter a client once and fire it across our full carrier panel, then get rates back from every market in one place, no rekeying and no bouncing between carrier sites. The first full end-to-end quote already came back with rates from every carrier, and I am hardening it now. We are not live yet, so do not lean on it for real quotes. But it is close, and when it lands it changes how fast we can quote for good.

Strategy desk

A quiet look at growing faster

Alongside everything we are building organically, we are quietly exploring whether acquiring other agency books could help us grow faster. Nothing is decided and nothing is announced. This is early and exploratory.

Part of that is exploring bringing in the right people to help build and develop a real mergers and acquisitions function here. The goal would be to make this a repeatable engine instead of a one-off: people whose job is to find good books, value them well, and bring them over cleanly. Early days, but it is the direction we are looking, and you are hearing it here first.

Here is why this is exciting for us specifically, and what it would actually mean for you. When most agencies buy a book, they inherit a mess: someone else's files, someone else's systems, and months of cleanup before anyone can sell. We would do the opposite, because we own CASEY. A book we bring on can be pulled straight into our own database, cleaned up, and put in front of you inside the same system you already use every day. The clients, the policies, the carriers, the renewals, all of it would land in one place we control. That means we could take on real volume without burying anyone in busywork, and it is a big part of why this path is even realistic for a shop our size. More to come when there is something real to share.

Congratulations

Eric Kelly, MBA

Master of Business Administration · University of Hartford · 2026

Eric graduated in May with his Master of Business Administration. We are proud of the degree, and prouder still of the person behind it. Over the last few years he has grown into our vice president and partner, the kind of teammate who makes everyone around him better and who this company is being built with, not just at. Congratulations, Eric. This one is earned, and it is only the start.

The University of Hartford feels the same way. They just published a Success Stories profile on Eric that traces his path from intern to vice president and partner at Safeshield, and how he is now creating internships and mentorship for the next group of young professionals coming up behind him. He called becoming a partner one of his proudest accomplishments, and we could not agree more. If you want proof of the growth, it is right here.

Read Eric's UHart Success Story →
New faces

The team keeps getting stronger

Justin Cronk
Agent · Live June 1
Justin went live as an agent on June 1 after months of training, showing up early and staying late to earn it. Three months of proving himself, and now it is official.
Trent Beggs
Summer intern · Getting licensed
Trent came to us from the University of Hartford, where he played lacrosse with Eric, and he is heading into his senior year. He joined for the summer as an intern and is getting licensed, with his sights set on becoming an agent.
Virtual Assistant · South Africa

Welcome, Marsha

Marsha is our virtual assistant, working with us from South Africa, and she keeps the back office moving: prepping quotes, handling servicing and follow-ups, and taking the busywork off the producers so they can stay in front of clients. She came in coachable and hungry, picked up our carriers and CASEY fast, and has already made the team's days lighter. We are lucky to have her, and this is only the beginning.

How CASEY speaks her language

Marsha has worked in insurance for a long time, but the terms in South Africa are a little different from ours. So we built CASEY to meet her where she is. The record stays in our American terms, and she can hover over any value to see CASEY translate it into her market: the currency, the products, the carriers, and the date format she already knows. Hover over the underlined values below to watch it happen.

This is Marsha's view. Hover over any underlined value to see how CASEY translates it for South Africa. On a phone, tap them.
ProductHomeowners (HO-3)
Coverage$321,000 dwelling
Premium$1,499 / year
Effective date06/19/2026
CarrierNarragansett Bay, preferred
The American record stays the single source of truth, and CASEY translates it into her market the moment she needs it. Under the hood that means a glossary that maps local carrier and coverage terms, carrier tier mapping to the right market hierarchy, currency conversion at a daily exchange rate, date and number formatting, and plain-English error messages, so nothing reads like a US-only system. The next hire abroad inherits all of it automatically.
A note of thanks

Twelve weeks ago, Lisa moved on. We did not slow down for a second. You doubled down, took on more, learned new tools, and kept the clients first while we rebuilt almost everything around them and built a stack of new things that were not here before. Everything in this briefing happened in that window. That is not luck, and it is not survival. That is a team moving fast and building something real.

Alyssa, Eric, Justin, Trent, Marsha, and everyone grinding through the day to day: thank you. The numbers at the top of this page are yours. This train is not slowing down, so let us go keep it climbing.

We are just getting started.
To the fucking moon!

Founder and CEO, Safeshield Insurance
Safeshield Insurance
